message:
feat(analytics): add server-side GA4 tracking via Measurement Protocol

Implement server-side analytics tracking to bypass ad blockers and
ensure reliable conversion tracking for wizard funnel analysis.

New API endpoint (apps/web/app/api/track/route.ts):
- POST /api/track: Accepts events and forwards to GA4 Measurement
  Protocol with proper payload structure
- GET /api/track: Health check endpoint for monitoring configuration
- Validates client_id and events array before forwarding
- Includes session_id derivation and engagement_time_msec for GA4
- Uses GA_API_SECRET server-side env var (not exposed to client)
- Returns appropriate error codes (400, 502, 503) for different failures

Analytics library updates (apps/web/lib/analytics.ts):
- Add getClientId(): Creates/retrieves persistent client ID from
  localStorage for consistent user identification across sessions
- Add sendServerEvent(): Async function that POSTs to /api/track
  with silent failure (doesn't disrupt UX if tracking fails)
- Update trackConversion(): Now uses dual-tracking strategy - sends
  to both client-side gtag (fast, may be blocked) AND server-side
  endpoint (reliable, bypasses ad blockers)

Analytics provider fix (apps/web/components/analytics-provider.tsx):
- Remove broken navigator.sendBeacon call that used Measurement
  Protocol with api_secret (can't safely expose secret client-side)
- Replace with gtag() call using transport_type: 'beacon' for
  reliable page exit tracking without exposing credentials
- Maintains all existing tracking (scroll depth, time on page)

This dual-tracking approach ensures critical conversion events
(wizard_start, wizard_complete, vps_created, installer_run) are
captured even when users have ad blockers enabled, providing
accurate funnel metrics for optimization.
